campaigns.title: SVX — Webinar Re-engagement
campaigns.targetAudience: Webinar partner orgs
campaigns.webinarAngle: Exclusivity — SVX investor audience gets early access to the Climate Tech Exit Lab framework before it goes public. BTC brings the content, SVX brings the list.
campaigns.ecosystemNote: SVX is a CAFIID member. Both Keyona and Bryan have equal social capital on this contact.
campaigns.waitingOn: James's response. Sent by Keyona Apr 6 2026.
campaigns.usageNote: Sent Apr 6 2026. Both Keyona and Bryan have equal social capital on this contact.
campaigns.emailSubject: Picking back up — webinar idea for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey James,

Hope the new year is treating you well. We connected late last year and I've been thinking about a collaboration that could be a good fit for your community.

We're putting together a webinar series on climate tech exits — breaking down how exits actually work in this space for small-check investors who want to understand the landscape before they commit capital. It's practical, framework-driven content that honestly doesn't exist anywhere else right now.

I'd love to co-host one session with SVX. You bring the audience, we bring the content. Low lift on your end, real value for your community.

Worth a 20-minute call to see if it makes sense?

Bryan

campaigns.emailSubject: Webinar collab — want to co-host one session?
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Bryan,

Hope you're feeling rested after the long weekend!

Bryan Duarte and I have been talking about doing more collaborative content this year, and he mentioned you and your work with both Clean Tech North and the Ontario Clean Tech Industry Association. We think there are some natural synergies there.

We're launching the Climate Tech Exit Lab this year — an initiative for both founders and investors on what goes into successful climate tech investments and exits. We'd love to co-host one session that we could bring to both communities.

Would you be open to a quick conversation to see if it's a fit?

Bryan

Hey [Name],

I'm Bryan — I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC.

I've been following CAFIID's work and noticed your Climate Finance Community of Practice — the work your members are doing sits right at the intersection of what we're building. We're launching the Climate Tech Exit Lab this year, focused on how climate tech investments and exits actually work, and I think your community would find real value in a session on it.

Worth a quick conversation?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Atmospheric Fund — ABM First Touch
campaigns.targetAudience: Institutional investors — Canadian
campaigns.contactIntel: Uzoma oversees the full deal cycle at TAF — market research, origination, structuring, due diligence, and investment approvals. TAF is a $100M endowment focused on low-carbon solutions in the Greater Toronto and Hamilton Area, using VC and project finance structures. Before TAF, Uzoma was Platform Manager at SVX, mobilizing institutional capital for impact investments. He holds an MBA from Schulich School of Business (York University). He also advises at Altitude Accelerator and has roots at York Angel Investors and MaRS. He thinks in deal structures. The angle for BTC is co-investment or deal flow referral, not a general intro.
campaigns.ecosystemNote: Bryan has a direct relationship with Uzoma. Warm — not cold. Uzoma's SVX background overlaps directly with BTC's webinar partner network.
campaigns.waitingOn: Sent to Bryan Apr 7 2026 for review. Bryan sending directly.
campaigns.usageNote: Email drafted and sent to Bryan Apr 7 2026. Bryan sends directly from his email.
campaigns.emailSubject: Still thinking about that SVX overlap
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Uzoma,

Hope Q2 is treating you well.

I've been following what TAF has been doing on the low-carbon investing side, and given where BTC sits — early-stage minority-led climate tech in Canada — I think there's something worth talking through for both of us around deal flow and what we're each seeing in the ecosystem.

Would you be up for 20 minutes sometime in the next few weeks?

Bryan

campaigns.title: Toronto Foundation — ABM First Touch
campaigns.targetAudience: Institutional investors — Canadian
campaigns.contactIntel: Rashid is COO at Toronto Foundation, which manages $758M in assets with $97M invested responsibly last year (9.9% return). He has 20+ years across finance, operations, and international development — previously spent two decades at Plan International leading global capital and operations initiatives. He's a Chartered Accountant and CMA. He sits on Toronto Foundation's investment committee as a staff member and is active in the Canadian philanthropy and impact investing space. campaigns.title: Thruline Thursdays — Exit Lab Guest / Cross-collab
campaigns.targetAudience: Founders, fund managers, operators — climate + deeptech
campaigns.cadence: First touch — cold outreach via Work on Climate Slack signal
campaigns.contactPerson: Casper (last name TBC — resolve via LinkedIn search 'Thruline Thursdays')
campaigns.exitLabAngle: Thruline Thursday 16 covered capital stack evolution — equity, venture debt, project finance, blended structures, LP dynamics. This is exactly the pre-exit territory the Exit Lab's deal teardowns complete. Their audience is founders and fund managers who need to understand exit mechanics.
campaigns.usageNote: Resolve full name and email via Apollo before sending. High priority — recurring monthly series with founder/operator audience.
campaigns.emailSubject: Your capital stack session + something we're building
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Casper,

I caught your March session on capital strategy surviving 2026 — the framing around how LP dynamics are shaping what deals actually move was exactly the kind of honest take that's hard to find.

I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC, and we're building something called the Climate Tech Exit Lab — a series that does deal-level teardowns of how climate tech exits actually happen. Not macro trend data, but the actual mechanics: what the acquirer paid for, what the capital path looked like, what the founder had to prove. It's the chapter after everything your audience is working through right now.

I'd love to explore two things: whether I could come on as a guest voice for a future Thruline Thursday, and whether any of the founders or fund managers from your March session would be strong interview subjects for the Exit Lab series.

Worth a quick conversation?

Keyona Meeks
Black Tech Capital

campaigns.emailSubject: Your February webinar — and a series we're building
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Liza,

Your February session with Shayle Kann and Gerard Reid was a great piece of framing for the year — the 'what actually scales' question is one the whole ecosystem is wrestling with.

I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC, and we're building the Climate Tech Exit Lab — a series that does deal-level teardowns of how climate tech exits actually happen from the inside. We're looking for investors who've been in the room on these deals and can speak to the mechanics, not just the macro.

Shayle's perspective on what a defensible climate tech exit looks like would be a strong fit for the series. I'd love to ask if you'd be open to making an intro, or if there's a natural moment where that conversation could happen.

Keyona Meeks
Black Tech Capital

campaigns.emailSubject: Your January climate tech investing panel
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Andrew,

I came across your January panel on the roller coaster of climate tech investing — the framing around whether the innovation wave has crested or is still building was exactly the question serious investors are sitting with right now.

I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC, and we're building the Climate Tech Exit Lab — a series that does deal-level teardowns of how climate tech exits actually happen. Keyona Meeks
Black Tech Capital

campaigns.title: Net Zero Angels — Guest Spot + Karen Sheffield Intro
campaigns.targetAudience: Angel investors — early-stage climate tech
campaigns.cadence: First touch — cold outreach via Work on Climate Slack signal
campaigns.exitLabAngle: Net Zero Angels is an angel investing community doing demo days for early-stage climate tech. Their February fireside featured Karen Sheffield of Pachamama Ventures. Their audience is small-check investors who are exactly who needs Exit Lab content — they're writing early checks without a clear picture of what the back end of the investment looks like.
campaigns.usageNote: Two asks: (1) guest spot at a future Net Zero Angels Demo Day, (2) intro to Karen Sheffield (Pachamama Ventures) as Exit Lab interview subject. Lead with the guest spot — it's mutually beneficial and lower friction than asking for an intro cold.
campaigns.emailSubject: Net Zero Angels + something we're building for your community
campaigns.emailDraft:
Hey Andrew,

I've been following what Net Zero Angels is building — the demo day model for early-stage climate tech is exactly the kind of infrastructure the ecosystem needs, and the February session with Karen Sheffield looked like a strong one.

I run Black Tech Capital, a climate tech nano VC, and we're building the Climate Tech Exit Lab — a series that does deal-level teardowns of how climate tech exits actually happen. We're focused on what the full arc looks like: from early check to liquidity event. That's the story your investors are trying to understand before they write the check.

I'd love to explore two things: whether there's a spot for a future Demo Day where the Exit Lab angle would resonate for your community, and whether Karen Sheffield would be open to a conversation for the series — her perspective on what makes an early-stage climate tech investment exit-ready would be exactly the kind of voice we're building around.

Keyona Meeks
Black Tech Capital
